edges.description: Recurrent fusion genes can drive tumorigenesis in fibrosarcoma subtypes
Malignant fibroblastic proliferation produces the localized soft-tissue tumor mass that characterizes infantile fibrosarcoma presentation.
NTRK fusion signaling creates a targetable dependency in fusion-positive tumors

nodes.description: TRK-inhibitor pharmacotherapy option for NTRK fusion-positive tumors, including the NTRK-translocated infantile fibrosarcoma context.

evidence.snippet: If NTRK gene fusions are detected, TRK inhibitors such as entrectinib and larotrectinib can be used regardless of the tumor entity.
Targeted neurotrophic tropomyosin receptor kinase (TRK) inhibitors offer a highly specific therapeutic option for patients with infantile fibrosarcoma (IFS) carrying the NTRK gene translocation.

nodes.description: Fibrosarcoma originates from fibroblast-lineage cells and behaves as a malignant neoplasm with proliferative spindle-cell growth.

evidence.snippet: Fibrosarcoma, originating from fibroblast cells, represents a malignant neoplasm that can manifest across all genders and age groups.

nodes.description: Recurrent fusion genes, especially ETV6-NTRK3 in infantile fibrosarcoma, create oncogenic kinase signaling that supports tumor growth and provides a therapeutic target.

evidence.snippet: Eighty-five percent of infantile fibrosarcomas are associated with t (12;15) (p13;25) chromosomal translocation resulting in ETV6-NTRK3 gene fusion, which provides the target for targeted therapy.
Many fusion genes underlie the pathogenic mechanisms triggering the onset of this disease.

nodes.description: Selective TRK-inhibitor pharmacotherapy for NTRK fusion-positive infantile fibrosarcoma and other NTRK-fused pediatric tumors.

evidence.snippet: Based on gene fusion targeted drug testing results, the patient was treated with standard doses of larotrectinib, resulting in significant mass shrinkage with no adverse effects, demonstrating the treatment effect of targeted therapy.
Radiologic review revealed objective tumor responses (OR) in 11 of 14 patients: Complete responses: two; partial responses: nine; and stable disease: three cases.

nodes.description: Infantile fibrosarcoma often presents as a localized painless mass that grows rapidly.

evidence.snippet: Infantile fibrosarcoma (IFS) is a rare tumor in childhood characterized by a single, localized, painless mass that grows rapidly but has a relatively indolent biological behavior and a favorable prognosis.

Here, we report a case of IFS in a newborn with a mass in the left lower extremity confirmed by imaging, histopathological examination, tissue FISH testing, and high-throughput sequencing to detect gene rearrangement.

nodes.description: NTRK-fusion infantile fibrosarcoma can remain dependent on constitutive TRK kinase signaling for tumor maintenance, making the tumor susceptible to TRK inhibitor pharmacotherapy.

evidence.snippet: Targeted neurotrophic tropomyosin receptor kinase (TRK) inhibitors offer a highly specific therapeutic option for patients with infantile fibrosarcoma (IFS) carrying the NTRK gene translocation.
TRKI have demonstrated very rapid responses in the vast majority of children with IFS with limited acute toxicity.
